questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: modern / has / technology / efficiency / improved / significantly
options: Modern technology has improved efficiency significantly
Efficiency has modern technology improved significantly
Improved efficiency has significantly modern technology
Technology modern has efficiency improved significantly
questions.solution: Modern technology has improved efficiency significantly

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Holt's method (double exponential smoothing):
options: Level: L_t = αD_t + (1-α)(L_{t-1}+T_{t-1}). Trend: T_t = β(L_t - L_{t-1}) + (1-β)T_{t-1}
questions.solution: Holt: captures trend. α=level smoothing, β=trend smoothing. Forecast: F_{t+m} = L_t + mT_t.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Slotting SKU affinity — association mining:
options: Apriori algorithm for items frequently ordered together. Position near each other in warehouse
questions.solution: Association: rules (A→B). Support (frequency), confidence (conditional probability). Pick correlation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RULA (Rapid Upper Limb Assessment) score interpretation:
options: Score 1-2: acceptable. 3-4: investigate. 5-6: change soon. 7: change immediately
questions.solution: RULA: upper limb disorders. Score based on posture, muscle use, force.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: CPM assumes:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Verification in simulation means:
questions.solution: Verification: debugging. Model does what intended. Validation: model represents reality.两者 distinct but complementary.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Micromotion study:
options: Film/video of operation. Frame-by-frame analysis. 16-24 frames/sec
questions.solution: Micromotion: therbligs analysis. Simultaneous motion cycle chart. 18 therblig symbols.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: A* algorithm is optimal when:
questions.solution: Admissible: h(n) ≤ h*(n). With admissible h, A* finds optimal. Consistent h is stronger: h(n) ≤ c(n,n')+h(n').

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Periodic review (R,S) — order up to:
options: Review every R periods, order up to S. S = μ_{R+L} + z·σ_{R+L}. Higher safety stock than (Q,R)
questions.solution: (R,S): time-phased. More safety stock (protects over R+L). Easier to administer (no cycle counting).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: VE — design for manufacture (DFM):
options: Simplify design, standardize parts, reduce part count. Assembly-friendly
questions.solution: DFM: Boothroyd-Dewhurst method. Minimum part count. Ease of handling, insertion.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Product layout (assembly line) suits:
questions.solution: Product layout: sequential arrangement. High throughput, low flexibility. Balance line: distribute work evenly.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sequencing — EDD:
options: Earliest Due Date. Minimizes maximum lateness. For due-date-driven shops
questions.solution: EDD: good for customer service (on-time delivery). SPT: good for throughput.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Condition-based maintenance (CBM): monitors:
questions.solution: CBM: maintain based on condition, not time. Reduces unnecessary maintenance. Requires sensors and analysis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the control chart shown (CL = 50, UCL = 62, LCL = 38), the highlighted point lying above the UCL indicates that the process is:
options: Out of control (an assignable cause is present)
In control with only chance variation
questions.solution: A point beyond the ±3σ control limits (UCL = 62) signals an assignable cause — the process is out of statistical control.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ergonomics — anthropometric percentile:
options: Design for 5th (female) to 95th (male) percentile. Adjustable for critical dimensions
questions.solution: Anthropometry: height, reach, grip span. Static (structural) vs dynamic (functional). Popliteal height.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Dynamic programming — backward induction:
options: Bellman equation: f_n(s) = min{x_n}[C_n(s,x_n) + f_{n+1}(s')]. Inventory, resource allocation, shortest path
questions.solution: DP: stages, states, decision, transition, return. Curse of dimensionality. Example: shortest path Floyd-Warshall.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Spaghetti diagram shows:
questions.solution: Spaghetti: trace movement on floor plan.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Discrete event simulation: events change:
options: System state at discrete points in time
questions.solution: DES: state changes at event times. Advance clock to next event. Process entities through system.统计 output.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Omnichannel supply chain:
options: Inventory pooling (ship-from-store), buy online pickup in store (BOPIS), dark stores
questions.solution: Omnichannel: inventory everywhere. Fulfillment from nearest node. Returns: any channel.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Value engineering: primary goal is to:
questions.solution: VE: systematic study of function vs cost. Function analysis. Eliminate unnecessary cost. Maintain required function.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An activity's normal time/cost are 10 days/₹3000 and crash time/cost are 4 days/₹8000. Its crash cost slope is:
questions.solution: Cost slope = (crash − normal cost)/(normal − crash time) = (8000−3000)/(10−4) = ₹833.3/day.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: OWAS (Ovako Working Posture Analysis) classifies:
questions.solution: OWAS: 4 body parts classified. Action categories 1-4: 1=normal, 4=correct immediately. Used for monitoring.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Duality in LP:
options: Primal (max) ↔ Dual (min). Dual variables = shadow prices
questions.solution: Duality: dual solution from primal simplex (z_j - c_j). Shadow price: value of relaxing one unit.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Drop shipping: retailer:
questions.solution: Drop ship: supplier ships directly to customer. No inventory risk for retailer. Lower margin, less control.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: EOQ — sensitivity:
options: Total cost near minimum for range around EOQ. Robust to estimation errors
questions.solution: EOQ: ±20% error → ~2% cost increase. SQRT(E) model. Total cost flat near EOQ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two components of reliability 0.9 and 0.7 are in parallel. The system reliability is:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Poka-Yoke (mistake-proofing) examples include:
options: Fixtures, guides, sensors that prevent errors
questions.solution: Poka-yoke: physical device prevents defects. Examples: asymmetric pins, guide rails, sensors. Eliminates human error.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost = ₹20000, price = ₹50/unit, variable cost = ₹30/unit. The break-even quantity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Supplier scorecards typically measure:
options: Quality, delivery, cost, responsiveness, and innovation
questions.solution: Scorecard: balanced view. Quality (PPM), delivery (OTD), cost (total cost), response (lead time).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Time study:
options: Observe, rate, apply allowances → standard time. Stopwatch + performance rating
questions.solution: Standard time = Normal time × (1 + allowance). Normal time = observed time × rating factor.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Simple moving average of order k: F_{t+1} = (D_t + D_{t-1} + ... + D_{t-k+1})/k. Larger k:
options: More smoothing, slower response to trend changes
questions.solution: MA(k): each observation has weight 1/k. Larger k → smoother. Lag: k/2 periods.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sensitivity — allowable increase/decrease:
options: Range for RHS coefficients in dual simplex. Range for objective coefficients in primal
questions.solution: Allowable increase: how much RHS can increase before shadow price changes. 100% rule.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Vendor classification system uses:
questions.solution: Kraljic matrix: profit impact vs supply risk.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: VE job plan phases:
options: Information → Creative → Evaluation → Development → Presentation → Implementation
questions.solution: VE: multi-disciplinary team. 40-hour workshop. 15-30% cost reduction typical.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An assembly line has total task time 48 min, 6 stations and a cycle time of 12 min. Its balance efficiency is:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: JIT (Just-in-Time) goals include:
options: Zero waste, zero defects, zero inventory
questions.solution: JIT: produce what is needed, when needed, in needed quantity. Eliminate waste. Requires stable demand, quality, suppliers.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Constraints Management: non-bottleneck resource:
questions.solution: Non-bottleneck: capacity > demand. Should not be run at full speed. Running full increases inventory.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Theory of Constraints: five focusing steps start with:
questions.solution: TOC steps: 1) Identify, 2) Exploit, 3) Subordinate, 4) Elevate, 5) Repeat. Start by finding bottleneck.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Hoshin Kanri (policy deployment) aligns:
options: Strategy from top to bottom of organization
questions.solution: Hoshin: catchball process. Top-level goals negotiated down. X-matrix shows linkages. Annual planning process.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Kanban system controls:
options: Production and material flow through visual signals
questions.solution: Kanban: pull system. Cards authorize production/movement. Limits WIP. Signals demand from downstream.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: (S-1, S) policy:
options: Order one when one is demanded. Base stock. For very expensive, slow-moving items
questions.solution: (S-1,S): S = base stock level. Essentially base stock with S > 1. Poisson demand.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With α = 0.2, last actual = 300 and last forecast = 220, the next exponential-smoothing forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bullwhip effect:
options: Demand amplification upstream. Causes: lead times, batching, promotions, rationing
questions.solution: Bullwhip: small retail variation → large factory swings. Mitigation: info sharing, VMI, ECR.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 1000 units, ordering cost = ₹50/order, holding cost = ₹8/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Earned value management (EVM):
options: EV = %complete × BAC. AC = actual cost. PV = planned value. SV = EV - PV. SPI = EV/PV. CV = EV - AC. CPI = EV/AC
questions.solution: EVM: SV>0 ahead, CPI<1 over budget. EAC = BAC/CPI. TCPI = (BAC-EV)/(BAC-AC) or (BAC-EV)/(EAC-AC).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sequencing rules — SPT:
options: Shortest Processing Time. Minimizes mean flow time, WIP. But long jobs may starve
questions.solution: SPT: optimum for mean flow time. EDD: minimize max lateness. CR: critical ratio.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transportation — unbalanced:
options: Supply ≠ demand. Add dummy row/col with zero cost
questions.solution: Dummy: surplus or shortage. Dummy row: excess supply = not shipped. Dummy col: unmet demand.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A worker's observed time is 6 min at a performance rating of 100%. The normal time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost = ₹20000, price = ₹50/unit, variable cost = ₹60/unit. The break-even quantity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: t-test — types:
options: One-sample (vs target), two-sample (two groups), paired (before-after). Assumes normality
questions.solution: t-test: for comparing means. F-test: for variances. ANOVA: 3+ groups. Non-parametric: Mann-Whitney, Kruskal-Wallis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Spare parts management:
options: Criticality, lead time, cost, obsolescence. Essential vs insurance vs capital spares
questions.solution: Spare parts: rotable, repairable, consumable. MTBS (mean time between supply). Spares optimization.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CRAFT (Computerized Relative Allocation of Facilities Technique):
options: Improvement heuristic. Pairwise department exchange to minimize material handling cost
questions.solution: CRAFT: starts with existing layout. Swaps departments. Converges to local optimum.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The last three demands were 120, 130 and 160 units. The 3-period moving-average forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bullwhip effect: one of the key solutions is:
questions.solution: Information sharing reduces uncertainty. VMI: vendor manages. CPFR: collaborative planning.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Integer programming: Gomory fractional cut:
options: For fractional basic variable x_i = b_i̅ (fractional part). Cut: Σ a_ij·x_j ≥ b_i̅
questions.solution: Gomory: adds constraint cutting off current fractional solution. Derived from simplex tableau row.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Parallel reliability: R₁=0.95, R₂=0.95. R_parallel=

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Little's law relates the average number in a system (L), arrival rate (λ) and time in system (W) as:
questions.solution: Little's law: average inventory/queue L = arrival rate × average time in system.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Vibration analysis: envelope analysis is used for:
options: Bearing defect detection (outer race, inner race, rolling element)
questions.solution: Envelope: demodulate high-frequency vibration. Envelope spectrum shows bearing defect frequencies.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transportation — prohibited routes:
options: Large cost M. Region blocked. Supplier cannot ship to certain destinations
questions.solution: Prohibited: assign very high cost. Algorithm will avoid. Infeasible if forced.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reliability of series system: R_s = R₁ × R₂ × ... × R_n. For 3 components each R=0.9:
questions.solution: Series: all must work. R_s = product of individual reliabilities. Decreases with more components.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CPM — independent float:
options: IF = max(0, ES_next - LF_current). Not affect previous or next activities
questions.solution: IF: = FF - delay of predecessors. Rarely used. Can be negative (ignore).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Parallel reliability: R₁=0.8, R₂=0.9. R_parallel=

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: LP — infeasible solution:
options: No point satisfies all constraints. Contradictory constraints
questions.solution: Infeasible: empty feasible region. Artificial variables in final basis. No solution.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Total Productive Maintenance (TPM) pillars:
options: Autonomous maintenance, planned maintenance, quality maintenance, focused improvement, etc.
questions.solution: TPM: 8 pillars. Autonomous: operators maintain. Planned: scheduled. Focused: improvement teams.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ANOVA in Gage R&R:
options: Part, operator, part×operator interaction, equipment (repeatability). F-tests, variance components
questions.solution: ANOVA: two-way with interaction. REML for variance components. AIAG MSA manual.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Layer cake scheduling (TOC):
options: Level 1: strategic (market). Level 2: tactical (due dates). Level 3: operational (capacity, DBR). Drum-buffer-rope at operational
questions.solution: Layer cake: strategy drives tactics drives operations. T/Ö = throughput per constraint unit.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Standard time = Normal time × (1 + Allowance%). For observed time=2min, rating=110%, allowance=15%:
questions.solution: Normal time = observed × rating = 2×1.10 = 2.20 min. Standard = 2.20×1.15 = 2.53 min.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A process has USL = 110, LSL = 90, mean = 100, σ = 2. Its Cpk is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Hungarian method is an efficient algorithm for solving the:
questions.solution: The Hungarian algorithm optimally assigns n jobs to n workers (one-to-one) at minimum cost.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Control chart — individual (X-MR):
options: For individual measurements. Moving range (MR) for variability. MR̄ = Σ|X_i - X_{i-1}|/(n-1)
questions.solution: X-MR: when sample size = 1. Automated inspection, slow processes. MR chart for σ estimate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bias in forecast: consistently over or under forecasting. Detected by:
options: Mean Error (ME) ≠ 0 or Tracking Signal outside control limits
questions.solution: ME = Σe_t/n. Positive: underforecast. Negative: overforecast. Should be close to zero.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Agile project management — Scrum:
options: Sprint (2-4 week), product backlog, sprint backlog, daily standup, review, retrospective. Velocity
questions.solution: Scrum: self-organizing team. PO (backlog), SM (process), Team (do the work). Burndown chart.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A line makes 800 units using 40 labour-hours. The labour productivity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MTBF=500h, MTTR=25h. Availability =

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DOE — full factorial:
options: 2^k experiments. All factor combinations. Main effects + interactions
questions.solution: 2^k: k factors, 2 levels each. Effect = Σ(+)/n_+ - Σ(-)/n_-. Interaction AB: 1/2×(effect of A at B+ minus B-).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Normal time is computed from the observed time using the:
questions.solution: Normal time = observed time × performance rating (rating normalizes worker pace).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Holt-Winters — additive seasonality:
options: Level + trend + seasonal. S = seasonal component. L_t = α(D_t - S_{t-s}) + (1-α)(L_{t-1}+T_{t-1})
questions.solution: Additive: when seasonal amplitude constant. Multiplicative: when seasonal amplitude proportional to level.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Exponential smoothing:
questions.solution: α: high = responsive, low = stable. Level only (no trend/seasonality). Simple smoothing.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The last three demands were 120, 150 and 160 units. The 3-period moving-average forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The last three demands were 100, 110 and 140 units. The 3-period moving-average forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DRP (Distribution Resource Planning):
options: Time-phased planning for distribution networks. Multi-echelon inventory
questions.solution: DRP: extends MRP principles to distribution. DRP record: gross requirements, scheduled receipts, projected on-hand.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For D = 6400/yr, S = ₹80, H = ₹2/unit/yr, the minimum total ordering-plus-holding cost (at EOQ ≈ 715.5) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FSN (Fast-Slow-Non moving):
options: By consumption rate. F: frequent. S: slow. N: non-moving (obsolete)
questions.solution: FSN: manage by turnover rate. F: lower safety stock (reliable). N: dispose or reduce.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The just-in-time (JIT) philosophy aims to:
options: Minimize inventory by producing only as needed
questions.solution: JIT pulls production to match demand, driving inventory (and waste) toward zero.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: LP — Big M method:
options: Artificial variables with large cost M. Minimizes M×sum of artificials
questions.solution: Big M: M >> other costs. Penalty for artificials in basis. M positive for min.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Group technology (GT):
options: Parts grouped into families by geometric/shape similarity. Cellular manufacturing
questions.solution: GT: Production Flow Analysis (PFA). Composite part concept. Cell formation algorithms.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Single-period model (newsvendor):
options: Service level = Cu/(Cu+Co). Cu = underage cost, Co = overage cost
questions.solution: Newsvendor: balance risk of overstocking vs understocking. Perishable goods, seasonal items.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost = ₹10000, price = ₹100/unit, variable cost = ₹50/unit. The break-even quantity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cognitive ergonomics: human error types:
options: Slip (wrong action), lapse (memory failure), mistake (wrong plan), violation (intentional)
questions.solution: Slip: correct plan, wrong execution. Mistake: wrong plan. Violation: intentional deviation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ISO 9000 series:
options: Quality management standards. ISO 9001:2015 — requirements. Process approach
questions.solution: ISO: 7 principles (customer focus, leadership, engagement, process, improvement, evidence, relationship).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 4, tm = 6, tp = 10 days. Its expected time is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Center of gravity method:
options: Optimal location minimizing weighted distance. x̄ = Σd_ix·W_i/ΣW_i
questions.solution: Center of gravity: coordinates weighted by volume. For distribution centers.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 5+6, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 2+10), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 11, 1-3-4 = 12. The critical path is the longest = 12 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Multiple activity chart:
options: Simultaneous activities of man + machine. Identify idle time. Gang chart
questions.solution: Multiple activity: man-machine ratio. Gang chart: team activities. Balance workload.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Work sampling: proportion of idle time p=0.3, e=0.05, 95% confidence:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Global supply chain — total landed cost:
options: Purchase price + freight + duty + insurance + inventory carrying + risk. Nearshoring calculation
questions.solution: TLC: used for sourcing decisions. Soft factors: IP risk, geopolitical stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A product is used at 20 units/day with a lead time of 5 days. The reorder point (no safety stock) is:
questions.solution: ROP = demand rate × lead time = 20×5 = 100 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operating Characteristic (OC) curve:
options: Probability of acceptance vs lot fraction defective. n and c determine shape
questions.solution: OC: AQL (α-risk), LTPD (β-risk). α = 1 - P_a at AQL. β = P_a at LTPD.
